remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: CIN2/3 (precancerous): spontaneous regression in 40-60% of CIN2 without treatment; CIN3: regression in ~30%; invasive cervical cancer: no spontaneous regression
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Early-stage (IA-IIA): radical hysterectomy or chemoradiation — 80-90% 5-year survival; locally advanced (IIB-IVA): cisplatin-based chemoradiation — 60-70% 5-year survival; metastatic with pembrolizumab + chemotherapy ± bevacizumab: 24.4 vs 16.3 months OS (KEYNOTE-826); ENGOT-cx11/GOG-3047: cemiplimab + chemo superior
remission.gap_size: Very large from a prevention standpoint — HPV vaccination and screening can prevent most cervical cancers but coverage is dramatically insufficient
remission.barrier_detail: HPV vaccination prevents ~90% of cervical cancers caused by HPV 16/18 (and gardasil 9 covers 9 types); cervical cancer screening (Pap + HPV co-testing) prevents invasive cancer; yet globally only 10% of women in LMICs have received the HPV vaccine; in the US, HPV vaccination coverage remains at ~60% of adolescents
remission.last_soc_change: 2022 — cemiplimab approved as first-line for PD-L1+ advanced/metastatic CC; 2021 pembrolizumab as first-line (KEYNOTE-826); tisotumab vedotin (ADC) 2021; cisplatin chemoradiation as standard since 1999
remission.outdated_treatments: Radical hysterectomy for locally advanced disease without chemoradiation (chemoradiation superior for stage IIB+); routine ovarian transposition in young patients for all locally advanced cases (individual assessment needed); delayed initiation of pembrolizumab while continuing bevacizumab alone in metastatic PD-L1+ disease
remission.adjunctive_emerging: HPV vaccination as prevention (catch-up to age 45); primary HPV testing replacing cytology (more sensitive for early detection); self-collected HPV samples (enabling screening in resource-limited settings); immune modulation strategies for CIN2/3 (topical imiquimod — Phase 2 data)
remission.frontier_signal: Tisotumab vedotin (HER2-ADC targeting tissue factor — Phase 3 innovaTV 301 positive); CXCL10/IP-10 pathway targeting; therapeutic HPV vaccines (ISA101, MEDI0457 — Phase 2 data); CAR-T targeting HPV oncoproteins E6/E7; self-sampling HPV testing expansion (eliminates cervical screening access barrier)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
